untranslatable_reason: This signal requires ranking stocks by 3-year historical volatility and constructing decile portfolios—a cross-sectional comparative analysis across ~2000 stocks. The backtest engine indicators are designed for single-stock time-series analysis. No allowed indicator can express 'rank all stocks by volatility and select top decile'. This requires portfolio-level construction logic outside the scope of individual condition evaluation.
translation_notes: Signal SSRN_980865_5 is a factor-based portfolio construction rule, not a traditional entry/exit signal. It requires: (1) computing 3-year weekly volatility for all FTSE World Developed constituents, (2) ranking them cross-sectionally, (3) selecting the lowest-volatility decile, (4) equal-weighting and monthly rebalancing. This is fundamentally a portfolio selection rule that cannot be reduced to indicator thresholds on individual securities. A proper backtest would need portfolio-construction hooks or a separate ranking/selection module.
